title: देवी स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी स्तुति ॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु विष्णुमायेति शब्दिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु चेतनेत्यभिधीयते।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु बुद्धिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥३॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु निद्रारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥४॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु क्षुधारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥५॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु छायारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥६॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥७॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु तृष्णारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥८॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु क्षान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥९॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु जातिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१०॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु लज्जारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥११॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१२॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु श्रद्धारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१३॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु कान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१४॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मीरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१५॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु वृत्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१६॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु स्मृतिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१७॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु दयारूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१८॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु तुष्टिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥१९॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु मातृरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२०॥

या देवी सर्वभूतेषु भ्रान्तिरूपेण संस्थिता।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२१॥

इन्द्रियाणामधिष्ठात्री भूतानां चाखिलेषु या।
भूतेषु सततं तस्यै व्याप्तिदेव्यै नमो नमः॥२२॥

चितिरूपेण या कृत्स्नमेतद्व्याप्य स्थिता जगत्।
नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥२३॥

सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२४॥

शरणागतदीनार्तपरित्राणपरायणे।
सर्वस्यार्तिहरे देवि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२५॥

॥ इति श्री मार्कण्डेयपुराणे देवीमाहात्म्ये देवी स्तुतिः सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Stuti ||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Vishnu-mayeti Shabdita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||1||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Chetanety-abhidhiyate.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||2||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Buddhi-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||3||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Nidra-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||4||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kshudha-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||5||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Chhaya-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||6||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shakti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||7||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Trishna-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||8||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kshanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||9||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Jati-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||10||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Lajja-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||11||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||12||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Shraddha-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||13||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Kanti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||14||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Lakshmi-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||15||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Vritti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||16||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Smriti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||17||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Daya-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||18||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Tushti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||19||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Matri-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||20||

Ya Devi Sarva-bhuteshu Bhranti-rupena Samsthita.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||21||

Indriyanam Adhishthhatri Bhutanam Chakhileshu Ya.
Bhuteshu Satatam Tasyai Vyapti-devyai Namo Namah.||22||

Chiti-rupena Ya Kritsnam Etad Vyapya Sthita Jagat.
Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.||23||

Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani Namo'stu Te.||24||

Sharanagata-dinarta-paritrana-paraayane.
Sarvasyarti-hare Devi Narayani Namo'stu Te.||25||

|| Iti Shri Markandeyapurane Devi-mahatmye Devi Stutih Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जो देवी सब प्राणियों में विष्णुमाया कहलाती हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार, नमस्कार, नमस्कार।

जो देवी सब प्राणियों में चेतना कहलाती हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार। जो देवी सब प्राणियों में बुद्धि रूप में स्थित हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार।

जो देवी सब प्राणियों में निद्रा, क्षुधा, छाया, शक्ति, तृष्णा, क्षान्ति, जाति, लज्जा, शान्ति, श्रद्धा, कान्ति, लक्ष्मी, वृत्ति, स्मृति, दया, तुष्टि, मातृ और भ्रान्ति रूप में स्थित हैं - उन सबको बारम्बार नमस्कार।

जो इन्द्रियों की अधिष्ठात्री हैं, समस्त भूतों में व्याप्त हैं, उन व्याप्ति देवी को नमस्कार। जो चिति रूप से सम्पूर्ण जगत को व्याप्त करके स्थित हैं, उन्हें नमस्कार।

हे सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये! हे शिवे! हे सर्वार्थसाधिके! हे शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरी! हे नारायणी! आपको नमस्कार है। हे शरणागत दीनों की रक्षा करने वाली! हे सबकी पीड़ा हरने वाली देवी नारायणी! आपको नमस्कार।
benefits: The most beloved and widely recited hymn to the Divine Mother
Invokes the Goddess in all her cosmic manifestations within all beings
Invokes wisdom, peace, strength, and divine grace
Awakens devotion and recognition of the Goddess in all creation
Invokes the blessing of all auspiciousness and fulfills all righteous purposes
Especially powerful during Navratri and Durga Puja celebrations
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Devi Stuti (देवी स्तुति) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Devi Mahatmyam (Markandeya Purana) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to awaken devotion and recognition of the Goddess in all creation and invoke the Goddess in all her cosmic manifestations within all beings. It is also said to bestow all auspiciousness and fulfill all righteous purposes. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. The text contains 25 verses and takes around 10 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ya Devi Sarvabhuteshu - Devi Stuti in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Most popular hymn to Goddess Durga from Devi Mahatmyam for Navratri.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Devi Stuti and what does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Stuti is a hymn of praise dedicated to the supreme Adi-Shakti — the cosmic Divine Mother who is simultaneously creator, sustainer and dissolver of the universe. It belongs to the family of stutis preserved within the Devi Mahatmyam (Durga Saptashati) of the Markandeya Purana, where the gods, sages and devotees praise the goddess at various points in the narrative. The hymn invokes Devi as Maha-Lakshmi (the embodiment of all auspiciousness), Maha-Saraswati (the embodiment of all wisdom), Maha-Kali (the embodiment of all power), Yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep), Maha-maya (the great illusion), Adi-shakti (the primordial energy), Mula-prakriti (the original nature), and the supreme Brahman in feminine form. It is the universal Devi paath of Shakta tradition. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam; Hindupedia Devi; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Devi Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Stuti is preserved within the Devi Mahatmyam of the Markandeya Purana — chapters 81 to 93 of that purana, also called the Durga Saptashati or Chandi Path, comprising 700 verses divided into thirteen chapters. Within this seven-hundred-verse text are several major stutis: the Brahma Stuti at the beginning (where Brahma praises Yoga-Nidra to wake Vishnu), the Sakaladi Stuti by the gods after Mahishasura's defeat, the Shakradi Stuti by Indra and the gods, and the famous Narayani Stuti in chapter 11. The Devi Stuti on this page belongs to this stuti corpus. Compilation texts such as Brihat Stotra Ratnakara and the daily Saptashati paath collections used in Bengali, Maharashtrian and South Indian Shakta households reproduce these stutis. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam; Hindupedia Markandeya Purana; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Devi Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday and Friday are the principal weekdays for any Devi stotra. Amavasya and Purnima are auspicious tithis. The supreme annual occasions are the two Navaratris — Sharad Navaratri in Ashvin (the most popular nine-night Devi festival culminating in Vijayadashami) and Chaitra Navaratri in spring (culminating in Rama Navami). Other peak occasions include Lalita Panchami, Durga Ashtami, Mahanavami, Akshaya Tritiya, Shakambhari Purnima, and the local Devi festivals of each region — Durga Puja in Bengal, Navaratri Garba in Gujarat, Bommai Golu in Tamil Nadu, Dussehra at Mysore. Devotees performing Saptashati paath recite the Devi Stuti as part of the daily Chandi Path during Navaratri. (Sources: Drikpanchang Navaratri; Hindupedia Durga Saptashati; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Devi Stuti phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources, especially the phalashruti chapter of the Devi Mahatmyam itself (chapter 12), promise sweeping rewards: protection from all fears and dangers, victory over enemies, destruction of disease and planetary affliction, prosperity and abundance, fertility and the birth of virtuous children, harmony in family life, freedom from possession by negative entities, success in righteous undertakings, and ultimately moksha through the maternal grace of the goddess. The chapter specifically promises that recitation during navaratri or on the eighth or ninth tithi of any month confers the merit of one thousand recitations. The stutis within the Saptashati are believed to be the most direct praise of the goddess and therefore especially efficacious. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam ch.12; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Devi Stuti recited as part of Saptashati Paath?
seoFaqs.answer: Saptashati Paath is the formal recitation of the entire 700-verse Devi Mahatmyam, performed either as Sampoorna Path (the entire text in one sitting, taking about 2-3 hours) or Khanda Path (divided across multiple days). The Devi Stutis appear at structurally significant points within the text: the Brahma Stuti opens the first charitra (chapter 1), the Sakaladi Stuti and Shakradi Stuti close the second charitra after Mahishasura's defeat, and the Narayani Stuti concludes the third charitra. During Navaratri, devotees often recite one charitra per three days, with the relevant stuti highlighted. The standard preliminaries include kavacha, argala and keelaka — the protective armours that surround the main text. (Sources: Hindupedia Saptashati Paath; Drikpanchang Navaratri; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Devi Stuti be recited by anyone, including children?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Pauranika stotra preserved in the open Markandeya Purana tradition, the Devi Stuti is accessible to all devotees regardless of gender, caste or age, and no formal diksha is required for daily recitation. Children across India are commonly taught the Devi Stuti as one of their first Devi prayers, often during Navaratri evening puja with the family. Women include it in the daily morning aarti and during Mangala Gauri vrat for family welfare. The only customary requirements are cleanliness of body and place, sitting facing east or north, and offering kumkum, haldi, red flowers (especially hibiscus), and naivedya of sweets before a Devi murti or image. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between Devi Stuti and Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Devi stotras but serve different functions. The Devi Stuti is a hymn of praise, invoking the goddess in her cosmic glory and asking for her grace and protection. The Devi Aparadha Kshamapana Stotra is a hymn of repentance, traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, in which the devotee acknowledges all the offences (aparadha) committed against the goddess through ignorance, negligence or wilful action and asks for her forgiveness (kshamapana). Devotees often recite both as a complete devotional cycle — the praise stuti invoking grace, then the kshamapana stotra apologising for any errors in the worship itself. The combination is especially recommended during Navaratri parayana and after long absences from regular devotional practice. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)
